experiments.hypothesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. In a fully choppy regime, the edge should come from broad reclaim-of-value behavior rather than rare continuation events.
experiments.expected_effect: abort: fail with QS 0.52, PF 0.62, DD 10.2%, trades 4
experiments.lesson_extracted: This ETH value-recovery scale-in branch traded, but it still failed honestly with PF 0.62, negative return, and only 4 trades. The partial improvement in transition bars does not rescue the fact that the tested edge is weak and too thin to trust in a fully choppy brief.

experiments.hypothesis: BTC 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing a range-compatible continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit indicators play different roles. In chop, the goal is not trend chasing; it is capturing the subset of holds that persist briefly before balance reasserts itself.
experiments.expected_effect: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.
experiments.lesson_extracted: This BTC asymmetric range-persistence branch produced 0 trades on valid data. Even with cleaner role separation, the entry grammar still overconstrained participation and did not engage at all.

experiments.hypothesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear quickly. In a choppy regime, the value is not in chasing trend; it is in participating only after a hold and cutting exposure fast if no expansion follows.
experiments.expected_effect: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.
experiments.lesson_extracted: This TAO retest-hold derisk branch also produced 0 trades on valid data. Time-based de-risking never had a chance to matter because the retest-hold continuation logic remained structurally too sparse for the lane.
